description_long: This object is a traditional hand saw with a straight, flat metal blade featuring a row of sharp, evenly spaced teeth along one edge. The blade is attached to a wooden handle, which is ergonomically shaped with a large opening for gripping and several metal fasteners securing it to the blade. The handle shows signs of wear and aging, with visible discoloration and some surface abrasions, suggesting frequent use. The blade itself is darkened, likely from oxidation or accumulated residue, and displays a slightly mottled texture. The teeth are fine and closely set, designed for manual cutting of wood or similar materials. There are no visible mechanical or powered components, and the overall design is utilitarian and robust, suitable for carpentry or general urban maintenance tasks.
description: A worn, traditional hand saw with a wooden handle and a dark, toothed metal blade, showing signs of heavy use.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the saw, with the wooden handle and toothed metal blade clearly visible.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the narrow profile of the blade and the thickness of the wooden handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the other face of the handle and blade.
description_view_3: End-on view from the opposite direction, again emphasizing the slimness of the blade and the handle's width.
attribute_handle_design: Single wooden handle with an ergonomic grip and a large opening for the hand, secured with metal fasteners.
